pattern: Prefix sums make any window's total O(1); sweeping once while remembering the best earlier window of each length lets you pair the two blocks in either order.
hints: Build prefix sums so the sum of any fixed-length window is a single subtraction.
Fix the rightmost window's end position; the other window must lie entirely to its left, so you need the best window of the other length ending before it.
Run two sweeps — one assuming the first-length block comes before the second, one the reverse — tracking the best earlier window as you go, and take the overall maximum.

edge_inputs.description: lengths 1 and 2, second block before first in the optimum
equal lengths, order does not matter but overlap must be avoided
best of each length are adjacent and cannot both be the global max window

stress.description: random non-negative array with valid window lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 600))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    f = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    s = rng.randint(1, n - f)
    return [nums, f, s]
